output.explanation: Mixed date-time format; most values are time-only like '9:40 a.m.' or '10:30 p.m.', while this includes a date prefix similar to other rare date-time variants.
Missing space before the date suffix; other values with this pattern use a space, e.g. '5:50 a.m. Dec 02' and '6:55 a.m. Dec 02'.
Date placed before the time, unlike the prevalent date-time pattern where the time comes first, e.g. '5:50 a.m. Dec 02'.
Mixed date-time format with a date prefix; the common structure for date-bearing entries is time followed by date, e.g. '6:55 a.m. Dec 02'.
Uses a slash-delimited date with a 4-digit year, unlike the rare date-bearing entries which are either time-only plus 'Dec 02' or slash dates with shorter forms.
Date suffix is attached without a space; comparable entries use a space before 'Dec 02', e.g. '12:21 a.m. Dec 02'.
Date-time entry uses a 2-digit year, while the other slash-format values here use a 4-digit year like '12/2/2011 6:55 a.m.'.
Date prefix format is inconsistent with the prevalent time-first date-bearing entries such as '5:50 a.m. Dec 02'.
Date suffix is attached without a space; the analogous entry '6:55 a.m. Dec 02' shows the expected spacing.
Date prefix format is inconsistent with the dominant time-first pattern among date-bearing values.
Time-only value amid date-bearing variants in this subset; the nearest matching date-bearing pattern is time followed by 'Dec 02'.
Date-time entry uses a compact date without year, unlike the more standard slash-form entries with year in this subset.
Date suffix is attached without a space; other similar values use '... a.m. Dec 02'.
Date-time entry uses a 2-digit year and a different date format than the other slash-based values with 4-digit year.
Date suffix is attached without a space; compare '12:21 a.m. Dec 02' and '6:55 a.m. Dec 02'.
The slash-based date format lacks the year present in the other slash-date entries, e.g. '12/2/2011 6:55 a.m.'.

Consistent with the dominant time-only pattern used by most values (e.g. '12:29 a.m.', '7:53 p.m.', '10:35 a.m.'); this one adds a weekday/date prefix in a different structure.
Most values are formatted as either time-only or time plus date in a consistent date style; this uses a different compact date format with a missing separator pattern ('11/30 7:53 p.m.').

Most entries use a standard time format with spaces and punctuation like 'a.m.'/'p.m.' or a clear date suffix; this value omits the space and punctuation in '12:29aDec 2', making it inconsistent with the prevalent patterns.
Most entries are time strings in formats like '12:29 a.m.' or 'Fri 02-Dec-2011' variants; this value adds a weekday/date prefix unlike the dominant time-only structure.
